# ISS-058: Unified artifact naming rubric across all pipeline-generated files

- **Type:** Architecture
- **Priority:** P2 — Medium
- **Label:** `framework`, `artifacts`, `naming`, `pipeline`, `conventions`
- **Created:** 2026-04-16
- **Reporter:** Linoy
- **Feature area:** Commands, skills, docs/CLAUDE.md conventions
- **Supersedes:** ISS-020

## Description

Pipeline-generated artifacts use inconsistent naming conventions. Core artifacts use bare names (`prd.md`, `architecture.md`, `review.md`), Codex review artifacts accidentally established a structured pattern (`review-codex-prd-<feature>.md`), and ISS-020 proposed yet another pattern (`<feature>-prd.md`). As the pipeline adds more review surfaces (ISS-057 phase-targeted reviews, ISS-053 multi-agent council), the naming inconsistency becomes a real problem:

- Agents can't programmatically discover artifacts without hardcoded paths
- Multiple reviews of the same phase overwrite each other (Phase 6 `review.md`)
- Editor tabs show generic names (`prd.md`, `review.md`) that are indistinguishable across features
- No convention distinguishes which agent produced an artifact

### Current state

| Artifact | Current name | Agent | Phase |
|----------|-------------|-------|-------|
| PRD | `prd.md` | product-owner | 1 |
| Architecture | `architecture.md` | architect | 2 |
| Security audit | `security-audit.md` | security-reviewer | 4 |
| Phase 6 review | `review.md` | code-reviewer | 6 |
| Codex PRD review | `review-codex-prd-<feature>.md` | codex | 1 |
| Codex architecture review | `review-codex-architecture-<feature>.md` | codex | 2 |
| Codex test design review | `review-codex-test-design-<feature>.md` | codex | 3 |
| Codex code review | `review-codex-code-<feature>.md` | codex | 5 |
| Claude test design review | `review-claude-test-design-<feature>.md` | claude | 3 |

### Proposed rubric

All pipeline-generated feature artifacts follow: `<type>-<agent>-<phase>-<feature>.md`

Or a simplified variant where agent/phase are optional for single-producer artifacts:
- `prd-<feature>.md` (only one PRD per feature, agent is always product-owner)
- `architecture-<feature>.md` (only one arch doc, agent is always architect)
- `review-<agent>-<phase>-<feature>.md` (multiple reviews possible, need agent + phase disambiguation)
- `security-audit-<feature>.md` (only one audit, agent is always security-reviewer)

The architect should decide the exact rubric — the key requirement is that it's consistent, programmatically parseable, and supports multi-agent multi-phase artifacts without collision.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1 (Naming convention defined):** A single artifact naming rubric is documented in `docs/CLAUDE.md` Code Conventions covering all pipeline-generated feature artifacts. The rubric must be consistent, programmatically parseable, and support disambiguation when multiple agents or phases produce artifacts of the same type.

- [ ] **AC2 (Commands updated):** All pipeline commands (`specify`, `architect`, `test-design`, `security-gate`, `implement`, `review`, `document`) use the new naming convention for artifacts they produce or consume.

- [ ] **AC3 (Discovery mechanism):** Agents and scripts can discover artifacts by pattern (e.g., glob `docs/features/<feature>/review-*.md` to find all reviews) without hardcoding specific filenames. The convention documents the discovery pattern for each artifact type.

- [ ] **AC4 (Multi-review support):** The naming convention supports multiple reviews of the same artifact type (e.g., Claude Phase 3 review + Codex Phase 3 review + Claude Phase 6 review) without collision.

- [ ] **AC5 (Backward compatibility):** Existing artifacts in `docs/features/*/` are either migrated or a compatibility shim maps old names to new. The migration strategy is documented.

- [ ] **AC6 (Status command updated):** `/status` discovers phase artifacts using the new naming patterns instead of hardcoded filenames.

- [ ] **AC7 (Checkpoint detection updated):** `checkpoint.js` `detectPhase()` uses the new naming patterns for phase detection.

- [ ] **AC8 (Contract tests):** Contract tests verify: (a) all commands reference the naming convention, (b) artifact discovery patterns work for each type, (c) multi-review naming doesn't collide.

- [ ] **AC9 (No regression):** Existing tests pass after migration.

## Notes

### Supersedes ISS-020

ISS-020 proposed `<feature>-prd.md` for PRD files only. This ticket covers all artifact types with a unified rubric. ISS-020 should be closed as superseded.

### Relationship to other tickets

- **ISS-020** — superseded (PRD naming only → unified rubric)
- **ISS-057** — phase-targeted reviews need phase-qualified artifact names. ISS-058 defines the naming convention ISS-057 would use.
- **ISS-053** — multi-agent review council produces multiple review artifacts per phase. ISS-058's multi-review support (AC4) is prerequisite for clean council artifact management.
- **ISS-006** — review-history.md depends on being able to discover and enumerate all review artifacts for a feature.
- **ISS-037** — additive review artifacts need predictable naming to expose the latest review.

### Design considerations

- The rubric must work for both single-producer artifacts (PRD, architecture) and multi-producer artifacts (reviews from different agents at different phases)
- Agent name in the filename enables artifact attribution without opening the file
- Phase number or phase name in the filename enables chronological ordering and phase-specific discovery
- Feature slug in the filename enables cross-feature search and editor tab disambiguation
- The migration from old names should be a one-time script, not a permanent compatibility layer
